message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Waiting For implementation ready for review">
The Waiting For feature implementation (Step 8 / US-GTD-5) is complete and all pipeline checks pass. Here is a summary for review:

## Implementation Summary

### Domain Layer (`src/domain/waiting_for_item.rs`)
- `WaitingForId(Uuid)` — newtype wrapper
- `WaitingOnPerson` — non-empty, max 100 chars, trimmed; constructed via `WaitingOnPerson::parse()`
- `WaitingForItem` enum — `Active` / `Completed` variants (same state machine pattern as NextAction/Project)
- 15 unit tests covering construction, validation, state transitions

### Infrastructure Layer (`src/infrastructure/waiting_for_repository.rs`)
- `insert_waiting_for_item`, `find_active_waiting_for_items_by_user`, `find_waiting_for_item_by_id`
- `complete_waiting_for_item`, `delete_waiting_for_item`, `count_active_waiting_for_items`, `update_waiting_for_item`
- Migration: `waiting_for_items` table with id, user_id (FK), title, waiting_on, created_at, completed_at

### Service Layer (`src/services/waiting_for_service.rs`)
- `add_waiting_for_item`, `list_active_waiting_for_items`, `count_active_waiting_for_items`
- `complete_waiting_for_item`, `delete_waiting_for_item`, `update_waiting_for_item`
- Error types: `AddWaitingForError`, `CompleteWaitingForError`, `DeleteWaitingForError`, `UpdateWaitingForError`
- All service functions have tracing instrumentation

### Routes (`src/routes/waiting_for.rs`)
- GET /waiting-for — list active items
- POST /waiting-for — add new item (title + waiting_on person)
- POST /waiting-for/{id}/complete — mark as received
- POST /waiting-for/{id}/delete — delete item
- GET /waiting-for/{id}/edit — get inline edit form (HTMX)
- POST /waiting-for/{id}/edit — update item
- GET /waiting-for/{id} — get single item fragment (for cancel edit)
- Full HTMX support with HX-Trigger announcements

### Templates
- `waiting_for.html` — main page with two-field add form ("What are you waiting for?" + "Who or what?"), empty state, semantic list
- `waiting_for_item.html` — item partial with Edit link, Received button, Delete button, all with aria-labels
- `waiting_for_edit.html` — inline edit form with title + waiting_on inputs, Save/Cancel, visually hidden labels

### Dashboard Integration
- Dynamic waiting_for_count displayed on dashboard stat card

### CSS (`static/css/main.css`)
- ~150 lines of waiting-for component CSS in @layer components
- Follows existing patterns (similar to projects, next-actions)

### Focus Management JS (`static/js/waiting-for-focus.js`)
- WCAG 2.4.3 compliant focus management for HTMX swaps
- Handles delete/complete (focus next/prev sibling), edit mode, empty state transitions

### Tests
- 85 unit tests (15 new for WaitingForItem domain)
- 220 integration tests (30 new for waiting-for routes)
- 11 Playwright e2e tests (all pass, no regression)

### Glossary (`docs/glossary.md`)
- Updated with WaitingForItem, WaitingForId, WaitingOnPerson types
- Added waiting-for actions, error types, state machine diagram, error copy convention

### Pipeline Status
- `cargo fmt --check`: PASS
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`: PASS
- `cargo test`: PASS (305 total)
- `npx playwright test`: PASS (11 e2e)

Please review and provide feedback. I am ready to commit once we have consensus.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good. I've now completed my full visual design review. Let me send my feedback.

input.content:
Steve Schoger here with my visual design review of the Waiting For implementation. I've reviewed all templates, the CSS additions, the dashboard integration, and the nav.

**Overall assessment: Very solid work.** The implementation follows the established visual patterns closely. CSS uses only design tokens, BEM naming is consistent, spacing matches the scale, and the page structure mirrors Next Actions and Projects. Here are my detailed notes:

---

**THINGS DONE WELL**

1. Page wrapper (`.waiting-for`) uses `padding-block-start: var(--space-lg)` -- matches Projects, Inbox, Next Actions. Consistent.

2. Error state (`.waiting-for__error`) is identical to `.projects__error` / `.next-actions__error` -- danger-light background, danger text, proper radius and spacing. Good.

3. Empty state (`.waiting-for__empty-state`) exactly matches the established pattern. Copy is clear and helpful.

4. List item borders (first-child top border + bottom border pattern) match all other list pages.

5. Action buttons (Edit link, Received button, Delete button) follow the exact same styling patterns as project items and next action items. Hover states are correct: primary for edit, success for received/complete, danger for delete.

6. Edit form follows the same save/cancel pattern as other inline edit forms. Font sizes and spacing are consistent.

7. Dashboard stat card for Waiting For integrates seamlessly with the existing grid.

8. Nav link is in the right position in the navigation order.

---

**Minor (non-blocking)**

1. **Waiting-on person display** -- The `.waiting-for-item__person` is styled as `font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-fg-muted)` which is fine, but I originally recommended a pill/badge treatment (background + border-radius) similar to `.next-action-item__context` to make the "who" scannable when scrolling. The current approach renders it as plain text below the title in a stacked layout via `.waiting-for-item__content` (flex-direction: column). This is actually a reasonable alternative -- it gives the item a two-line card feel with title on top and metadata below. Both approaches work. The stacked layout may actually be better here since waiting-on text can be longer than context names. **No change required** -- just noting the design rationale is sound.

2. **Date color** -- `.waiting-for-item__date` uses `--color-fg-subtle` (gray-400, ~3:1 ratio). This is the correct token for tertiary information that is not body text. Good use of the hierarchy: title at full `--color-fg`, person at `--color-fg-muted`, date at `--color-fg-subtle`.

---

**No blocking issues.** The visual design is clean, consistent with the established patterns, uses only design tokens, and maintains clear visual hierarchy across the three data levels (title, person, date). The add form with two field groups is well-structured.

I approve this for consensus.
input.summary: Visual design review: approved, no blocking issues

**Overall assessment: Very solid work.** The implementation follows the established visual patterns closely. CSS uses only design tokens, BEM naming is consistent, spacing matches the scale, and the page structure mirrors Next Actions and Projects. Here are my detailed notes:

---

**THINGS DONE WELL**

1. Page wrapper (`.waiting-for`) uses `padding-block-start: var(--space-lg)` -- matches Projects, Inbox, Next Actions. Consistent.

2. Error state (`.waiting-for__error`) is identical to `.projects__error` / `.next-actions__error` -- danger-light background, danger text, proper radius and spacing. Good.

3. Empty state (`.waiting-for__empty-state`) exactly matches the established pattern. Copy is clear and helpful.

4. List item borders (first-child top border + bottom border pattern) match all other list pages.

5. Action buttons (Edit link, Received button, Delete button) follow the exact same styling patterns as project items and next action items. Hover states are correct: primary for edit, success for received/complete, danger for delete.

6. Edit form follows the same save/cancel pattern as other inline edit forms. Font sizes and spacing are consistent.

7. Dashboard stat card for Waiting For integrates seamlessly with the existing grid.

8. Nav link is in the right position in the navigation order.

---

**Minor (non-blocking)**

1. **Waiting-on person display** -- The `.waiting-for-item__person` is styled as `font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--color-fg-muted)` which is fine, but I originally recommended a pill/badge treatment (background + border-radius) similar to `.next-action-item__context` to make the "who" scannable when scrolling. The current approach renders it as plain text below the title in a stacked layout via `.waiting-for-item__content` (flex-direction: column). This is actually a reasonable alternative -- it gives the item a two-line card feel with title on top and metadata below. Both approaches work. The stacked layout may actually be better here since waiting-on text can be longer than context names. **No change required** -- just noting the design rationale is sound.

2. **Date color** -- `.waiting-for-item__date` uses `--color-fg-subtle` (gray-400, ~3:1 ratio). This is the correct token for tertiary information that is not body text. Good use of the hierarchy: title at full `--color-fg`, person at `--color-fg-muted`, date at `--color-fg-subtle`.

**No blocking issues.** The visual design is clean, consistent with the established patterns, uses only design tokens, and maintains clear visual hierarchy across the three data levels (title, person, date). The add form with two field groups is well-structured.

I approve this for consensus.

content.text:
I've completed my visual design review and sent approval to Luca. Summary of my review:

**Approved -- no blocking issues.** The implementation is visually consistent with the established design system:

- All spacing uses design tokens from the scale
- BEM class naming follows the established convention
- Page structure matches Projects, Next Actions, and Inbox patterns exactly
- Three-level visual hierarchy is well executed: title (full color), person (muted), date (subtle)
- Error, empty state, action buttons, edit form, and dashboard integration all follow existing patterns precisely
- No hardcoded values, no new design tokens needed

The only minor observation was that the waiting-on person is displayed as stacked text rather than the pill/badge I originally suggested, but the stacked approach is actually better given that waiting-on text can be longer than context names. Sound design decision.
